title: Bundle Overview
tagline: Cite Evidence | Support Claims | Analyze Text
standard_alignment_content: RI.6.1: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text. Students learn to reference specific details and examples from informational texts to justify their understanding.
standard_breakdown_content:
What students must know: Difference between explicit and implicit information, what constitutes valid textual evidence, how to identify supporting details.
What students must do: Quote or paraphrase evidence, make logical inferences, connect evidence to analysis.
Required skills: Close reading, analytical thinking, citation techniques.
Depth of knowledge: Level 2-3 analysis and reasoning.
whats_included_content:
1. Bundle Overview Page
2. Vocabulary Pack
3. Anchor Reading Passages
4. Reading Comprehension Questions
5. Writing Prompt Pack
6. Graphic Organizer Pack
7. Mini-Lesson & Anchor Chart
8. Exit Tickets
9. Short Quiz
10. Performance Task
11. Homework Page
12. Final Review Page + Answer Key
learning_objectives_content:
• Identify explicit information in informational texts
• Draw logical inferences from textual clues
• Cite specific evidence to support claims
• Distinguish between strong and weak evidence
• Analyze author's purpose using text support
• Write evidence-based responses
suggested_pacing_content:
Day 1: Introduce explicit vs. implicit information concepts
Day 2: Practice identifying and citing textual evidence
Day 3: Making inferences with text support activities
Day 4: Evidence evaluation and analysis practice
Day 5: Performance task and assessment completion
materials_needed_content:
• Printed reading passages and worksheets
• Highlighters in multiple colors
• Sticky notes for text annotations
• Chart paper for anchor charts
• Digital access for online passages
• Student notebooks or binders
differentiation_tips_content:
For Struggling Readers: Provide shorter passages, pre-highlight key sections, use graphic organizers for evidence tracking, offer sentence starters for responses.
For Advanced Learners: Include complex texts, require multiple pieces of evidence, add synthesis across sources, create original analysis questions.
For ELL Students: Include visual supports, provide vocabulary pre-teaching, allow native language discussion, use collaborative grouping strategies.
assessment_overview_content:
Formative: Daily exit tickets, evidence scavenger hunts, peer discussions with observation checklists.
Summative: Performance task requiring multi-paragraph analysis with cited evidence, comprehensive quiz on standard components.
Standards-Based: Rubrics aligned to RI.6.1 proficiency levels measuring citation accuracy and inference quality.
